questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A Thread is passing through a hole at the centre of a frictionless table. At the upper end a block of mass 0.5 kg is tied and a block of mass 8 kg is ties at the lower end which freely hanging. The smaller mass is rotated on the table with a constant angular velocity about the axis passing through the hole so as to balance the heavier mass. If the mass of the hanging block is changed from 8 kg to 1 kg . The fractional change in the angular velocity of the smaller mass is $-\frac{\mathrm{K}}{4}$, so that it balances the hanging mass again. Find the value of K .
questions.solution: For circular motion of a body tied to a string on a horizontal plane $\frac{m v^2}{r}=T$ here, $\mathrm{T}=\mathrm{mg}$ $\begin{aligned} & \frac{m v_1^2 / r_1}{m v_2^2 / r_2}=\frac{M_1 g}{M_2 g} \\ & \frac{v_1^2 r_2}{v_2^2 r_1}=\frac{8}{1}...(i) \end{aligned}$ Now as angular momentum is conserved about pt 0 . $m v_1 \mathrm{r}_1=m v_2 \mathrm{r}_2....(ii)$ from (i) and (ii) $\frac{r_1}{r_2}=2$ $\begin{aligned} & \text { further } \frac{\omega_2}{\omega_1}=\frac{v_2}{v_1} \times \frac{r_1}{r_2}=\left(\frac{r_1}{r_2}\right)^2 \\ & \frac{\omega_2}{\omega_1}=\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^2=\frac{1}{4} \\ & \frac{\Delta \omega}{\omega}=\frac{\omega_2-\omega_1}{\omega_2}=\frac{\omega_2}{\omega_1}-1=\frac{1}{4}-1=-\frac{3}{4}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A uniform rod of mass $M$ is released from horizontal position as shown in the figure. The rod is free to rotate about a horizontal frictionless axis passing through hinge $P$. The reaction from the hinge just after the release is $\mathrm{Mg} / \mathrm{K}$. Find the value of K .
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \alpha=\tau / \mathrm{l}=\left(\mathrm{Mg} \frac{\mathrm{L}}{2} / \frac{\mathrm{ML}^2}{3}\right)=\frac{3}{2} \frac{\mathrm{~g}}{\mathrm{~L}} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{cm}}=\alpha \mathrm{L} / 2=3 \mathrm{~g} / 4=\frac{\mathrm{Mg}-\mathrm{N}}{\mathrm{M}} \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{N}=\frac{\mathrm{Mg}}{4}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A carpet runner is placed onto a long straight inclined plane parallel to the steepest line in the plane, from the bottom of the slope till its top. The carpet is thin and flexible, and it cannot slide down because of the friction, but it can easily be lifted, it does not stick to the plane. The top end of the carpet is rolled, a bit, and this hoop of carpet is released. The hoop rolls down the slope with greater and greater speed, while its diameter increases, and finally it reaches the bottom of the slope during a time of $t_1$. If a solid cylinder is released at the top of the slope, it reaches the bottom in a time of $\mathrm{t}_2$. The ratio of $\frac{t_1}{t_2}=\sqrt{k}$. Find $k$.
questions.solution: The rolled part of the carpet is a cylinder, and its mass is proportional to $x$. Applying conservation of mechanical $\left(1+\frac{1}{2}\right) \frac{1}{2}(\lambda x)\left(\frac{d x}{d t}\right)^2=(\lambda x) g \frac{x \sin \theta}{2}$ energy, $\begin{aligned} & \therefore \left(\frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{dt}}\right)^2=\mathrm{v}^2=\frac{2 \mathrm{~g} x \sin \theta}{3} \\ & \text { i.e. } a_{\text {carpet }}=\frac{\mathrm{g}}{3} \end{aligned}$ For the cylinder, $\frac{3}{4} m v^2=m g x \sin \theta$ $i.e. a_{c y 1}=\frac{2 g}{3}$ $\frac{t_1}{t_2}=\sqrt{\frac{a_{\mathrm{cy} 1}}{a_{\text {carpet }}}}=\sqrt{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: There is a rectangular plate of mass M kg of dimensions $(\mathrm{a} \times \mathrm{b})$. The plate is held in horizontal position by striking $n$ small balls each of mass $m$ per unit areaper unit time. These are striking in the shaded half region of the plate. The balls are colliding elastically with velocity v . What is v ? It is given $\mathrm{n}=100, \mathrm{M}=3 \mathrm{~kg}$, $\mathrm{m}=0.01 \mathrm{~kg} ; \mathrm{b}=2 \mathrm{~m} ; \mathrm{a}=1 \mathrm{~m} ; \mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$
questions.solution: Taking torque about axis $\mathrm{OO}^{\prime} \&$ putting it equal to zero $\begin{aligned} & \left(\vec{\tau}_{\text {net }}\right)_0=0 \\ & \mathrm{mg} \frac{\mathrm{b}}{2}-\mathrm{F} \frac{3 \mathrm{~b}}{4}=0\end{aligned}$ $\mathrm{F}=\frac{2 \mathrm{mg}}{3}$ $\ldots(i)$ $\mathrm{F}=\mathrm{n}(2 \mathrm{mv})\left(\frac{\mathrm{ab}}{2}\right)$ $\ldots(ii)$ From (i) \& (ii), putting the values we get $\mathrm{v}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Portion AB of the wedge shown in figure is rough and BC is smooth. A solid cylinder rolls without slipping from $A$ to $B$. Find the ratio of translational kinetic energy to rotational kinetic energy, when the cylinder reaches point C .
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \mathrm{mgh} & =\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2+\frac{1}{2} \frac{\mathrm{mR}^2}{2} \omega^2 \\ \mathrm{mgh} & =\frac{3}{4} \mathrm{mv}^2\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & 2 \mathrm{mgh}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_{\mathrm{f}}^2+\frac{1}{2} \frac{\mathrm{mR}^2}{2} \omega^2 \\ & 2 \mathrm{mgh}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_{\mathrm{f}}^2+\mathrm{mgh}-\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2\end{aligned}$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{mgh}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_{\mathrm{f}}^2-\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2 \\ & \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_{\mathrm{f}}^2=\frac{5}{3} \mathrm{mgh}\end{aligned}$ $\therefore$ Ratio $=\frac{\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_{\mathrm{f}}^2}{\frac{1}{2} \frac{\mathrm{mv}^2}{2}}=\frac{\frac{5}{3} \mathrm{mgh}}{\frac{1}{4} \frac{4}{3} \mathrm{mgh}}=5$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Figure shows the variation of the moment of inertia of a uniform rod, about an axis passing through its centre and inclined at an angle $\theta$ to the length. The moment of inertia (in $\mathrm{kg}-\mathrm{m}^2$ ) of the rod about an axis passing through one of its ends and making an angle $\theta=\frac{\pi}{3}$ will be
questions.solution: $\mathrm{I}=\frac{\mathrm{ML}^2}{12} \sin ^2 \theta \Rightarrow 1=\frac{\mathrm{ML}^2}{12} \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right) \Rightarrow \mathrm{ML}^2=12$ $\mathrm{I}=\frac{\mathrm{ML}^2}{3} \sin ^2 \theta, \text { at } \theta=\frac{\pi}{3}, \mathrm{I}=\frac{\mathrm{ML}^2}{3}\left(\frac{3}{4}\right)=\frac{\mathrm{ML}^2}{4}=\frac{12}{4}=3 \mathrm{~kg}-\mathrm{m}^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A disc shaped body (having a hole as shown in the figure) of mass $\mathrm{m}=10 \mathrm{~kg}$ and radius $\mathrm{R}=\frac{10}{9} \mathrm{~m}$ is performing pure rolling motion on a rough horizontal surface. In the figure point $O$ is geometrical center of the disc and at this instant the centre of mass $C$ of the disc is at same horizontal level with O . The radius of gyration of the disc about an axis passing through $C$ and perpendicular to the plane of the disc is $\frac{R}{2}$ and at the instant shown the angular velocity of the disc is $\omega=\sqrt{\frac{g}{R}} \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{sec}$ in clockwise sense. g is gravitation acceleration $=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$. Find angular acceleration $\alpha\left(\right.$ in rad $\left/ \mathrm{s}^2\right)$ of the disc at this instant.
questions.solution: considering torque of real and pseudo force in the frame of $P$, $\begin{aligned} & \tau=m g \frac{R}{2}+m \omega^2 R \frac{R}{2} \\ & =m g \frac{R}{2}+m \frac{g}{R} R \frac{R}{2}=m g R \\ & I_p=m\left(\frac{R}{2}\right)^2+m\left(R^2+\frac{R^2}{4}\right)=\frac{3}{2} m R^2 \\ & \therefore \alpha=\frac{2 m g R}{3 m R^2}=\frac{2 g}{3 R}=6 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}^2 \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Water flowing along an open channel drives an undershot waterwheel of radius 2 m (figure). The water approaches the wheel with a speed of $5.0 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and leaves with a speed of $2.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$; the amount of water passing by is 200 kg per second. At what rate does the water deliver angular momentum to the wheel $($ in kJ$)$ ?
questions.solution: $\frac{d L}{d t}=\frac{d m}{d t}\left(v_1-v_2\right) R=200 \times(5-2.5) \times 2=1000 \mathrm{~J}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A bullet of mass $m$ moving with velocity $v_0(-\hat{k})$ strikes the bottom of a stationary vertical uniform ring of same mass m and radius $\mathrm{R}=1 \mathrm{~m}$. The ring lies in XY plane with its topmost point hinged on the ceiling. The ring can rotate about X -axis. There is no friction between the hinge and the ring. The bullet gets embedded in the ring immidiately after collision. Find the angular velocity of the system (in radian $/ \mathrm{s}$ ) just after collision. [Take $v_0=11 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ ]
questions.solution: $\mathrm{mv}_0 \cdot 2 \mathrm{R}=\left[\frac{3}{2} \mathrm{mR}^2+\mathrm{m}(2 \mathrm{R})^2\right] \omega ; \omega=\frac{4 \mathrm{v}_0}{11 \mathrm{R}}=4 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $A$ uniform rod $O A B$ is bent in $L$ shape to form right angle at $A$. Length of $O A$ is $L$ and that of $A B$ is $\frac{L}{2}$ respectively as shown in figure. The rod is hinged at the end $O$ and is free to rotate in a vertical plane about $O$. It is set free from rest, when larger section of it is in horizontal position. Maximum angular acceleration of rod is $\frac{\sqrt{65}}{\mathrm{~K}} \mathrm{~g}$. The value of K is
questions.solution: Let A, B, and C be C.M. of larger section, smaller section and total rod $\begin{aligned} & C^{\prime}=\frac{L}{12}, A C^{\prime}=\frac{\mathrm{L}}{6} \\ & O C=\left\{\left(\frac{\mathrm{L}}{2}+\frac{\mathrm{L}}{6}\right)^2+\left(\frac{\mathrm{L}}{12}\right)^2\right\}^{\frac{1}{2}}= \\ & \frac{\sqrt{65}}{12} \mathrm{~L}\end{aligned}$ Moment of inertia about ' $O$ ' $\mathrm{I}=\frac{7}{8} \mathrm{ML}^2 \Rightarrow$ Maximum torque of about 'O' $\begin{array}{r}\tau_{\max }=\frac{3 \mathrm{Mg}}{2} \times(\mathrm{OC}) \\ \alpha_{\max }=\frac{\tau_{\max }}{\mathrm{I}}=\frac{\sqrt{65}}{7}\left(\frac{\mathrm{~g}}{\mathrm{~L}}\right)\end{array}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A uniform solid sphere rolls down on a vertical rigid surface without slipping. The vertical surface moves with an acceleration $\mathrm{a}=\mathrm{g} / 2$ in horizontal direction as shown. The minimum coefficient of friction between the sphere and vertical surface so as to prevent slipping is $\mu$. Write $14 \mu$ as answer.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{mg}-\mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{s}}=\mathrm{m}\left(\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{cm}}\right)_{\mathrm{rel}} \cdots(1) \\ & \mathrm{N}-\frac{m g}{2}=\mathrm{m}(0) ; \mathrm{N}=\frac{m g}{2} ; \mathrm{f}_{\mathrm{s}} \times \mathrm{R}=\frac{2}{5} \mathrm{mR}^2 \alpha\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A solid cylinder rolls without slipping along a horizontal plane with velocity $66 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and reaches a plane inclined at $60^{\circ}$ with the horizontal. If there is sufficient friction to prevent slipping then the body starts rolling up the inclined plane with the velocity v. Find the value of $v(i n m / s)$.
questions.solution: Initial angular momentum of the solid sphere about $\mathrm{B}=\mathrm{I} \omega+\mathrm{m} v_{\mathrm{cm}} \mathrm{r} \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{MR}^2}{2} \frac{\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{C}}}{\mathrm{R}}+\mathrm{mv}_{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{R} \cos \theta$ Final angular momentum of the solid sphere at $B=m v^2 R+\frac{M R^2}{2} \frac{v^{\prime}}{R}$ Angular momentum about the $B$ will remain conservation $L_i=L_F$ Angular momentum about the B will remain conservation
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle starts moving in a non-uniform circular motion, has angular acceleration as shown in figure. The angular velocity at the end of 4 radian is given by $\omega \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$ then find the value of $\omega$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \alpha=\frac{\omega d \omega}{d \theta} \\ & \int \omega d \omega=\int \alpha d \theta \\ & \frac{\omega^2}{2}=\operatorname{area} \text { under } \alpha \text { vs } \theta \text { graph }=\frac{1}{2}(9 \times 4) \\ & \omega=\sqrt{36}=6 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A horizontally uniform rod $A B$ of mass $m$ and length $\ell_0$ rotates freely about a stationary vertical axis OO' passing through the end A , which is located exactly midway between O and $\mathrm{O}^{\prime}$ and $\mathrm{OO}^{\prime}=\ell$. At what angular velocity (in units) of the rod is the horizontal component of the force acting on the lower end of the axis OO' equal to zero. ( $\mathrm{g}=10$ $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^2, \ell=5 \mathrm{~m}, \ell_0=3 \mathrm{~m}$ )
questions.solution: About O $\mathrm{N}^{\prime} \ell+\tau_{\text {centrifugal }}+\left(-\mathrm{mg} \frac{\ell_0}{2}\right)=0$ About $\mathrm{O}^{\prime}$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{N} \ell+\tau_{\text {centrifiugal }}+\left(-\mathrm{mg} \frac{\ell_0}{2}\right)=0 \\ & \tau_{\text {centrifugal }}=\int_0^{\ell} \frac{\mathrm{m} \omega^2 \ell}{2 \ell_0} \mathrm{xdx}=\frac{1}{4} \mathrm{~m} \omega^2 \ell \ell_{\circ} \text { (anticlockwise) } \\ & \therefore \mathrm{N}^{\prime} \ell+\frac{1}{4} \mathrm{~m} \omega^2 \ell \ell_0-\mathrm{mg} \frac{\ell_0}{2}=0 \\ & \text { and } \mathrm{N} \ell-\frac{1}{4} \mathrm{~m} \omega^2 \ell \ell_0-\mathrm{mg} \frac{\ell_0}{2}=0 \\ & \text { When } \mathrm{N}^{\prime}=0, \frac{1}{4} \mathrm{~m} \omega^2 \ell \ell_0-\mathrm{mg} \frac{\ell_0}{2}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \omega=\sqrt{\frac{2 \mathrm{~g}}{\ell}} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A solid cylinder C and a hollow pipe P of same diameter are in contact when they are released from rest as shown in the figure on a long incline plane. Cylinder C and pipe P roll without slipping. Determine the clear gap (in m) between them after 4 seconds.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & a_c=\frac{g \sin \theta}{1+\frac{I}{m R^2}}=\frac{g \sin \theta}{1+\frac{1}{2}}=\frac{2}{3} g \sin \theta \quad a_P=\frac{g \sin \theta}{1+\frac{I}{m R^2}}=\frac{g \sin \theta}{2} \\ & a_{\text {rel }}=\frac{g \sin \theta}{6}=\frac{10 \times \frac{3}{5}}{6}=1 ; s_{r e l}=\frac{1}{2} \times 1 \times 16=8 \mathrm{~m}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A uniform stick of mass 18 kg and length 3 m spins around on a frictionless horizontal plane, with its Centre of Mass stationary. A mass M is placed on the plane, and the stick collides elastically with it, as shown (with the contact point being the end of the stick). What should M (in kg ) be so that after the collision the stick has translational motion, but no rotational motion?
questions.solution: (i) $\mathrm{L}_{\mathrm{i}}=\mathrm{L}_{\mathrm{f}} ; \frac{\mathrm{m} l^2}{12} \omega=\mathrm{MV} \times \frac{l}{2} \Rightarrow \mathrm{~V}=\frac{\mathrm{m} \omega l}{6 \mathrm{M}}$ (ii) $\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{i}}=\mathrm{P}_{\mathrm{f}} ; 0=\mathrm{MV}-\mathrm{mV}_1 \Rightarrow \mathrm{~V}=\frac{\mathrm{mV}}{\mathrm{M}} \Rightarrow \frac{\omega l}{6}=\mathrm{V}_1$ (iii) $\mathrm{e}=1=\frac{\mathrm{V}-\left(-\mathrm{V}_1\right)}{\omega \frac{l}{2}-0} \Rightarrow \mathrm{~V}+\mathrm{V}_1=\frac{\omega l}{2} ; \frac{\mathrm{m} \omega l}{6 \mathrm{M}}+\frac{\omega l}{6}=\frac{\omega l}{2} ;\left(\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{M}}+1\right)=3$ $\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{~m}}{\mathrm{M}}=2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{M}=\frac{\mathrm{m}}{2}=9 \mathrm{~kg}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A capstan is a rotating drum (cylinder) over which a rope or cord slides in order to increase the tension due to friction. If the difference in tension between the two ends of the rope is 500 N and the capstan has a diameter of 10 cm and rotates with angular velocity $10 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$. Capstan is made of iron and has mass 5 kg , specific heat $1000 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{kg} \mathrm{K}$. At what rate does temperature rise ? Assume that the temperature in the capstan is uniform and all the thermal energy generated flows into it. Express your answer as $\mathrm{x} \times 10^{-4}{ }^{\circ} \mathrm{C} / \mathrm{s}$. Fill up value of $x$.
questions.solution:
(a) The net torque that the rope exerts on the capstan, and hence the net torque that the capstan exerts on the rope, is the difference between the forces of the ends of the rope times the radius of the capstan.
The capstan is doing work on the rope at a rate $P=\tau \omega=F_{\text {net }} r \frac{2 \pi \text { rad }}{T}=(520 \mathrm{~N})\left(5.0 \times 10^{-2} \mathrm{~m}\right)$ $\frac{2 \pi \mathrm{rad}}{(0.90 \mathrm{~s})}=182 \mathrm{~W}$, or 180 W to two figures. A larger number of turns might increase the force, but for given forces, the torque is independent of the number of turns.
(b) $\frac{\Delta \mathrm{T}}{\mathrm{t}}=\frac{\mathrm{Q} / \mathrm{t}}{\mathrm{mc}}=\frac{\mathrm{P}}{\mathrm{mc}}=\frac{(182 \mathrm{~W})}{(6.00 \mathrm{~kg})(470 \mathrm{~J} / \mathrm{kg} \cdot \mathrm{k})}=0.064 \mathrm{C}^{\circ} / \mathrm{s}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the arrangement shown the rod is freely pivoted at point $O$ and is in contact with the equilateral triangular block which can moves on the horizontal frictionless ground. As the block is given a speed v forward, the rod rotates about point $O$. Find the angular velocity of rod in rad/s at the instant when $\theta=30^{\circ}$. [Take $v=20$ $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}, \mathrm{a}=1 \mathrm{~m}]$
questions.solution: $\mathrm{AB}=\mathrm{v} d t \sin \theta$ ...(i) (along the circular arc) $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{OA}=\frac{\mathrm{AN}}{\sin \theta} \\ & \mathrm{ds}=\mathrm{rd} \theta \end{aligned}$ given vdt $\sin \theta=\mathrm{OA} d \theta$ $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{vdt} \sin \theta=\frac{\mathrm{AN}}{\sin \theta} \cdot \mathrm{d} \theta \\ & \mathrm{vdt} \sin \theta=\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\sin \theta} \mathrm{d} \theta \\ & \frac{\mathrm{d} \theta}{\mathrm{dt}}=\frac{\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{a}} \sin ^2 \theta \\ & \frac{\mathrm{d} \theta}{\mathrm{dt}}=5 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In the figure shown, mass of the plank is m and that of the solid cylinder is 8 m . Springs are light. The plank is slightly displaced from equilibrium and then released. Find the period of small oscillations (in seconds) of the plank. There is no slipping at any contact point. The ratio of the mass of the plank and stiffness of the spring i.e. $\frac{\mathrm{m}}{\mathrm{K}}=\frac{2}{\pi^2}$
questions.solution: $\mathrm{Kx}-\mathrm{f}_1=\mathrm{m} \mathrm{a}_2$ ....(i) $f_1-f_2=8 m a_1$ ....(ii) $\tau_{\mathrm{cm}}=\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{cm}} \alpha$ or $\left(f_1+f_2\right) R=\frac{(8 m) R^2}{2} \alpha$ $\left(\mathrm{f}_1+\mathrm{f}_2\right)=\frac{(8 \mathrm{~m}) \mathrm{a}_1}{2}$ ....(iii) From (ii) and (iii), $2 f_1=(8 m) \times \frac{3 a_1}{2}$ or $\quad f_1=(8 \mathrm{~m}) \times \frac{3 a_1}{4}$ and $\mathrm{f}_2=2 \mathrm{ma}_1$. Hence, $\mathrm{f}_2$ will be in forward direction. Also, for no slipping, at the point of contact between plank and cylinder. $2 a_1=a_2$ ....(iv) $\mathrm{f}_1=(8 \mathrm{~m}) \times\left(\frac{3 \mathrm{a}_2}{8}\right)=(3 \mathrm{~m}) \mathrm{a}_2$ ....(v) from (i) and (v), $2 K x-(3 m) a_2=m a_2$ $\mathrm{a}_2=\frac{2 \mathrm{~K}}{4 \mathrm{~m}} \mathrm{x}$ or $\left(\frac{-d^2 x}{d t^2}\right)=\left(\frac{2 K}{4 m}\right) x$ or $\frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{x}}{\mathrm{dt}^2}=-\left(\frac{2}{4} \times \frac{\pi^2}{2}\right) \mathrm{x}$ or $\quad \omega^2=\frac{\pi^2}{4}$ or $\quad \omega=\frac{\pi}{2}$ or $\quad \frac{2 \pi}{T}=\frac{\pi}{2}$ or $\mathrm{T}=4$ seconds.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two uniform spheres shown in figure rotate separately on parallel axis. The upper sphere has angular speed $\omega_0$ and lower sphere is at rest. Now the two spheres are moved together so that their surfaces touch. After a short time the two spheres now in contact are rotating without slipping. Find the final rate of rotation of upper sphere in rad/s. [given moment of inertia of upper sphere $I_1=4 \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~m}^2$, moment of inertia of lower sphere $\mathrm{I}_2=2 \mathrm{~kg} \mathrm{~m}^2, \mathrm{a}=2 \mathrm{~m}, \mathrm{~b}=$ $\left.1 \mathrm{~m}, \omega_0=12 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}\right]$.
questions.solution:
When the two disc come in contact, they exert equal and opposite forces.
$\int$ Fadt $=I_1\left(\omega_0-\omega_1\right)$    ....(i)
$\int \mathrm{Fbdt}=\mathrm{I}_2 \mathrm{\omega}_2$    ....(ii)
finally $\omega_1 \mathrm{a}=\omega_2 \mathrm{~b}$
$l_1\left(\omega_0-\omega_1\right)=l_2 \frac{a^2}{b^2} \cdot \omega_1$
$\omega_1=\frac{l_1 \omega_0}{l_1+\frac{a^2}{b^2} l_2}=4 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A uniform cylinder of radius $R(=3 \mathrm{~m})$ is spin about its axis at an angular velocity $\omega_0(=40 \sqrt{\pi} \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{sec})$ and placed between two perpendicular wall. The coefficient of friction between the walls and cylinder is $\mu(=2)$. Then, 25 K turns will the cylinder make before it stops. Find the value of K.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mu \mathrm{N}_1+\mathrm{N}_2=\mathrm{mg}...(i) \\ & \mathrm{N}_1=\mu \mathrm{N}_2....(ii) \\ & \mathrm{~N}_2=\frac{\mathrm{mg}}{\left(1+\mu^2\right)} \\ & \mathrm{N}_1=\frac{\mu \mathrm{mg}}{\left(1+\mu^2\right)}\end{aligned}$ Torque about centre of mass $\begin{aligned} & \mu\left(N_1+N_2\right) R=\frac{1}{2} m R^2 \alpha \\ & \alpha=\frac{2 \mu\left(N_1+N_2\right)}{m R}=\frac{2 \mu(1+\mu) g}{\left(1+\mu^2\right) R} \\ & \omega^2=\omega_0^2-2 \alpha \theta \\ & \theta=\frac{\omega_0^2}{2 \alpha} \\ & \theta=\frac{\omega_0^2\left(1+\mu^2\right) R}{2 \times 2 \mu(1+\mu) g}\end{aligned}$ Hence number of turns $\begin{aligned} & N=\frac{\theta}{2 \pi}=\frac{\omega_0^2 R\left(1+\mu^2\right)}{4 \mu(1+\mu) g \cdot 2 \pi} \\ & N=\frac{\omega_0^2 R\left(1+\mu^2\right)}{8 \mu \pi g(1+\mu)}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Disc of mass $M=10 \mathrm{~kg}$ and radius $R=50 \mathrm{~cm}$ is revolving about fixed vertical axis on smooth horizontal surface with angular velocity $\omega_0=4 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{sec}$. A block of mass $\mathrm{m}=5 \mathrm{~kg}$ is connected to disc through slack string as shown in the diagram. Find the velocity of the block when string become taut.
questions.solution:
Angular momentum Conservation
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{1}{2} M R^2 \omega_0=\left(\frac{M R^2}{2}+m R^2\right) \omega^{\prime} \\
& \left(\frac{M}{M+2 m}\right) \omega_0=\omega^{\prime} \\
& U=\omega^{\prime} R=\left(\frac{M}{M+2 m}\right) \omega_0 R
\end{aligned}$
